perturbation.original_text: 5. Information shall be deemed the property of the disclosing Party and, within ten (10) business days upon written request from the disclosing party, the other party will return all such information received in tangible form to the disclosing party or will destroy all such information.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Information shall be deemed the property of the disclosing Party and, within thirty (30) business days upon written request from the disclosing party, the other party will return all such information received in tangible form to the disclosing party or will destroy all such information.
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies the timeframe for returning or destroying confidential information upon request. Initially, the receiving party had ten business days. The modified text extends this period to thirty business days. This directly contradicts the timeline implied in Section 10, which requires prompt action for disclosures required by law. If a subpoena demands immediate disclosure and return of documents, the 30-day return window becomes problematic. If a legal order requires an immediate action, waiting 30 days becomes in-text contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. In the event that the receiving party or its employees or Representatives (a) need (for securities law purposes) to make disclosures of information or (b) are required by law, regulations, or government agency or court orders, interrogatories, requests for information or documents, subpoenas, or civil investigative demands to disclose any information, in the case of (a) the receiving party shall provide the disclosing party with prompt written notice so that the disclosing party can work with the receiving party to limit the disclosure to the greatest extent possible consistent with legal obligations (it being understood that disclosure of the name of the other party will never be made without that party's prior written consent); or in he case of (b) the receiving party shall use its reasonable efforts to minimize such disclosure and obtain an assurance that the recipient shall accord confidential treatment to the information, and shall notify the disclosing party contemporaneously of such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the timeframe for returning confidential information from 10 to 30 business days. The contradicted text (Section 10) requires 'prompt' written notice and reasonable efforts to minimize disclosure, including in cases of subpoenas or court orders demanding immediate disclosure. A 30-day return window directly contradicts the requirement for prompt action in Section 10, creating a clear in-text contradiction as per the 'Inconsistencies' perturbation definition, which highlights conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: 15. This Agreement shall become effective as of the date of which it is first executed below ("Effective Date"), provided that this Agreement shall cover all information disclosed by one party to the other whether before or after the Effective Date. Disclosures of information under this Agreement may take place for a period (the "Information Disclosure Period") of two (2) years after the Effective Date. The obligations of the parties contained in Paragraphs 5, 10 and 12 shall survive and continue beyond the expiration of the Information Disclosure Period by a further period of two (2) years.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. This Agreement shall become effective as of the date of which it is first executed below ("Effective Date"), provided that this Agreement shall cover all information disclosed by one party to the other whether before or after the Effective Date. Disclosures of information under this Agreement may take place for a period (the "Information Disclosure Period") of one (1) year after the Effective Date. The obligations of the parties contained in Paragraphs 5, 10 and 12 shall survive and continue beyond the expiration of the Information Disclosure Period indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: This change reduces the Information Disclosure Period from two years to one year but extends the survival of obligations in Paragraphs 5, 10, and 12 indefinitely. This creates an in-text contradiction: while new disclosures are limited to one year, the obligations to return information (Paragraph 5), comply with legal disclosures (Paragraph 10), and restrict transmission (Paragraph 12) now last forever. This contradicts the intent of a defined Information Disclosure Period, as the confidentiality and return obligations continue even after the ability to disclose new information has expired. It creates uncertainty regarding whether information disclosed within the initial one-year period is subject to perpetual obligations, or if those obligations apply only to that specific initial disclosure period. This also impacts section 17 because, the survival clause now creates uncertainty regarding the scope of the 'entire understanding' as it effectively amends the terms indefinitely, contradicting the 'entire understanding' clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17. This Agreement (a) constitutes the entire understanding between the parties with respect to information provided in connection with the Transactions, (b) supersedes all prior agreements between the parties with respect to information provided in connection with discussions relating to the Transactions and (c) shall bind each party with respect to all information received by it prior to the expiration of the Information Disclosure Period. No amendments or modification of this Agreement shall be valid or binding on the parties unless made in writing and executed on behalf of each party by its duly authorized representative.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 15 to reduce the Information Disclosure Period from two years to one year while simultaneously extending the survival of obligations in Paragraphs 5, 10, and 12 indefinitely. This directly contradicts section 17, which states that the agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the parties with respect to information provided in connection with the Transactions and binds each party with respect to all information received by it prior to the expiration of the Information Disclosure Period. The indefinite survival of obligations after a defined Information Disclosure Period now creates a contradiction. Since the changed text extends obligations indefinitely, it conflicts with Section 17's assertion that the agreement binds each party with respect to information received prior to the expiration of the Information Disclosure Period, as those obligations now effectively last forever regardless of the period's expiration, creating an in-text inconsistency, which aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, where conflicting deadlines or obligations are established within the same document.

perturbation.original_text: 3. All information which is disclosed by one party to the other in connection with discussions relating to the Transactions, whether before or after the date of execution of this Agreement, shall automatically be deemed proprietary or confidential and subject to this Agreement unless otherwise confirmed in writing by the disclosing party. In addition, the existence and terms of this Agreement, and the fact and substance of Company's discussions and correspondence with Broker relating to the Transactions, including the identification of either party by name or identifiable in connection with the parties' participation in such process, shall be deemed information of both parties and shall not be disclosed by either party without the consent of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. All information which is disclosed by one party to the other in connection with discussions relating to the Transactions, whether before or after the date of execution of this Agreement, shall automatically be deemed proprietary or confidential and subject to this Agreement unless otherwise confirmed in writing by the disclosing party. The existence and terms of this Agreement shall be deemed information of both parties and shall not be disclosed by either party without the consent of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes a clause that deems 'the fact and substance of Company's discussions and correspondence with Broker relating to the Transactions...shall be deemed information of both parties and shall not be disclosed by either party without the consent of the other party'. This creates a contradiction with Section 8, which states 'Each party retains the right, in its sole discretion, to determine whether to disclose its information to the other party.' By removing the clause in Section 3, a party could argue that the substance of the discussions are no longer automatically confidential, which contradicts the underlying assumption of Section 8 that all information is confidential unless affirmatively disclosed. This creates uncertainty about which communications are protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Each party retains the right, in its sole discretion, to determine whether to disclose its information to the other party, and disclosure of information of any nature shall not obligate the disclosing party to disclose any further information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit confidentiality of 'the fact and substance of Company's discussions and correspondence with Broker relating to the Transactions'. This directly contradicts Section 8, which assumes all information is subject to a party's discretion of disclosure. By removing the automatic confidentiality of discussions, the changed text creates an inconsistency regarding what information is protected, which is a critical inconsistency and a contradiction based on the definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: 16. Each party agrees that the disclosing party wold be irreparably injured by a breach of this Agreement by the receiving party or its Representatives and that the disclosing party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance, in the event of any breach of the provisions of this Agreement. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, but shall be addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. Each party agrees that the disclosing party would be irreparably injured by a breach of this Agreement by the receiving party and that the disclosing party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance, in the event of any material breach of the provisions of this Agreement. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, but shall be addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies the conditions for equitable relief. The original clause states that equitable relief is available for *any* breach. The modified version limits it to *material* breaches. This creates an in-text contradiction with Paragraph 4. If a representative makes an unauthorized disclosure, the availability of equitable relief depends on whether the court considers it a 'material' breach. This weakens the disclosing party's remedies for minor violations outlined in paragraph 4, but contradicts the statement in paragraph 16 that the injured party is entitled to relief. This creates legal uncertainty about which standard of breach determines the remedy and impacts Paragraph 4 about obligations, as it provides the level of enforcement that will come after a breach has been made.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. With respect to information disclosed under this Agreement, the party to whom the information is disclosed and its Representatives shall: a. hold the information in confidence, exercising a degree of care not less than the care used by such party to protect its own proprietary or confidential information that it does not wish to disclose, and in no event less than a reasonable degree of care; b. restrict disclosure of the information solely to those Representatives with a need to know and not disclose it to any other person; c. advise those Representatives of their obligations with respect to the information; and d. use the information only in connection with continuing discussions by the parties concerning the Transactions, except as may otherwise be mutually agreed upon in writing, and shall reproduce such information only to the extent necessary for such purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that equitable relief is available for *any* breach, while the changed text limits it to *material* breaches. This creates a direct contradiction with Paragraph 4, which outlines obligations related to confidential information. Paragraph 4 does not specify whether breaches of those obligations must be 'material' to trigger equitable relief. The changed text introduces a requirement of 'materiality' that was not present before, thus directly conflicting with the original intent and enforceability of Paragraph 4.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights by license or otherwise in any information disclosed, or under any trademark, patent, copyright, mask work or nay other intellectual property right of either party. None of the information which may be disclosed or exchanged by the parties shall constitute any representation, warranty, assurance, guarantee or inducement by either party to the other of any kind, and, in particular, with respect to the non-infringement of trademarks, patents, copyrights, mask works or any other intellectual property right.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights by license or otherwise in any information disclosed, or under any trademark, patent, copyright, mask work or nay other intellectual property right of either party. Information exchanged by the parties relating to technical improvements on existing products shall constitute an inducement by the disclosing party to the other to explore potential collaboration opportunities. None of the information which may be disclosed or exchanged by the parties shall constitute any representation, warranty, assurance, guarantee or inducement by either party to the other of any kind, and, in particular, with respect to the non-infringement of trademarks, patents, copyrights, mask works or any other intellectual property right.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence, 'Information exchanged by the parties relating to technical improvements on existing products shall constitute an inducement by the disclosing party to the other to explore potential collaboration opportunities,' creates a contradiction with the preceding and following sentences. The paragraph initially states that nothing grants rights or constitutes an inducement. By adding this new sentence, it introduces the idea that certain information *does* constitute an inducement for collaboration, conflicting with the explicitly stated intention not to grant rights or assurances. It specifically introduces an inducement, when the whole section is trying to prevent that from being the case. This could create uncertainties related to Section 7, specifically regarding the parties' commitment to future transactions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Neither this Agreement, nor the disclosure of Information under this Agreement, nor the ongoing discussions and correspondence by the parties concerning the Transactions or any other matter, shall constitute or imply any promise or intention to make any purchase or use of products, facilities or services by either party or its affiliated companies or any commitment by either party or its affiliated companies with respect to any other present or future transaction. If, in the future, the parties elect to enter into binding commitments relating to the Transactions or any transaction, such commitments will be explicitly stated in a separate written agreement executed by both parties, and the parties hereby affirm that they do not intend their discussions, correspondence, and other activities to be construed as forming a contract relating to the Transactions or any other transaction without execution of such separate written agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 11 introduces a direct contradiction with both the original intent of Section 11 and with Section 7. The original Section 11 explicitly states that nothing in the agreement should be construed as granting rights or inducements. The added sentence creates an exception by stating that information regarding technical improvements constitutes an inducement, conflicting with the preceding statement. Furthermore, Section 7 states that discussions and correspondence shall not constitute a commitment to future transactions. By introducing an inducement in Section 11, the changed text implies a potential commitment to explore collaboration opportunities, which directly contradicts Section 7's assertion that no such commitment exists without a separate written agreement. This qualifies as a strong contradiction, as it creates uncertainty about whether the parties are committed to future transactions based on the exchange of information.
